docs.title: XGen - Framework
docs.text: XGen - Framework is an open source project providing a framework and basic resources for the HELIX platform. The main goal is a well documented, bug free and easy to adapt framework for developers to build on.

docs.title: DBSC
docs.text: The DBSC (Database Synchronized Class) class provides a simple way to synchronize classes and objects with the database.

docs.title: DBSC.Meta
docs.text:
DBSC.Meta.Column

fields name - string The name of the table representing this class in the database. columns - table<DBSC.Meta.Column> The columns of table in the database.

docs.title: DBSC.Meta.Column
docs.text: fields name - string the name of the column. This is the name of the column in the database and must be the name of the value in the class. type - string the type of the value. This can be a normal database type e.g. VARCHAR or the class name of another DBSC class. primary_key - boolean? if true this column will be registered as primary key. not_null - boolean? if true this column will be registered as not nullable. unique - boolean? if true this column cannot have the same value twice.

docs.title: DBSC:new(meta)
docs.text:
DBSC.Meta Creates a new class extending the DBSC super class.

parameters meta - DBSC.Meta the metadata for the database table

returns class - T the new class extending the dbsc class.

example

MyClass = DBSC:new({
    name = "my_class",
    columns = {
        { name = "identifier", type = "VARCHAR", primary_key = true }
    }
})

docs.title: DBSC:init()
docs.text:
DBSC:new(meta) Initializes a class extending the DBSC super class. This will create the table in the database (if not exists). This requires the 'self' object to be a DBSC subclass, created with DBSC:new(meta)

example

-- after class creation and database initialization.

MyClass:init()

docs.title: DBSC:update()
docs.text:
Updates the row in the database with the data of this object.

returns success - boolean true if the object was successfully updated in the database, false if something went wrong (e.g. database not initialized, class not initialized, row deleted).

docs.title: DBSC:get(primary_keys)
docs.text:
*Loads an object by its primary keys from the database of this subclass of the DBSC super class. This will save the object in the cache for the given primary keys, so when this function is called *

parameters primary_keys - table<string, any> the values of the primary keys to get the object of this class for.

returns obj - T? the object for the given primary keys or nil if the object doesn't exist.

example

local obj = MyClass:get({ identifier = "my_identifier" })

docs.title: DBSC:getWhere(conditions)
docs.text:
DBSC:get(primary_keys)

Loads a list of objects of this class where the given conditions are met. This will NOT put this object in the cache and should therefor be cached manually or when ever possible DBSC:get(primary_keys) be used instead.

parameters conditions - table<string, any> the conditions that have to be met in order for the row to be selected. The key must be a valid column of the table in the database and the value must match the type of that column. The conditions are AND connected.

returns objects - table<T> The objects that match the given conditions.

docs.title: DBSC:delete()
docs.text:
Deletes this object from the database and cache.

returns success - boolean true if the row was deleted, false otherwise.

example

-- myObject must be an object of a class extending the DBSC class.
myObject:delete()
